tasks.title: Browser install, config schema, and install.sh update for webkit/firefox
definition_of_done: reference-impl/config/ui-review.json schema accepts new top-level 'browsers' key (array of engine strings, default ["chromium"]), 'axe.browser' key (string, default "chromium"), and 'maxCombinations' key (integer, default 90).
reference-impl/install.sh runs 'npx playwright install webkit firefox' after the existing chromium step, and on Linux also runs 'npx playwright install-deps webkit'.

Install documentation (reference-impl/README.md or equivalent) is updated to document the webkit/firefox install step, the PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH convention, and the approximate additional disk footprint (~300–350 MB).

A JSON Schema or TypeScript type for UiReviewConfig is updated to include 'browsers', 'axe.browser', and 'maxCombinations', with backward-compatible defaults so existing consumer configs (e.g. .cloverleaf/config/ui-review.json) that omit these keys continue to work.
Unit tests verify that a config omitting the new keys resolves to the correct defaults (browsers=["chromium"], axe.browser="chromium", maxCombinations=90).
acceptance_criteria: A reference-impl/config/ui-review.json with only legacy keys passes config validation and resolves browsers to ["chromium"], axe.browser to "chromium", and maxCombinations to 90.
A config explicitly setting browsers to ["chromium","webkit","firefox"] and maxCombinations to 45 passes validation.
A config setting axe.browser to "webkit" passes validation.
install.sh executed on a Linux machine runs both 'npx playwright install webkit firefox' and 'npx playwright install-deps webkit' without error (verified via script inspection or integration test).
install.sh executed on macOS does not run 'npx playwright install-deps webkit' (Linux-only guard is present).
The README or install docs state the aggregate disk footprint (~600–650 MB total) and the PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=~/.cache/ms-playwright convention.

tasks.title: Baseline layout migration: git-mv flat chromium baselines to baselines/chromium/ subdirectory
definition_of_done: All existing flat .cloverleaf/baselines/{slug}-{viewport}.png files are moved to .cloverleaf/baselines/chromium/{slug}-{viewport}.png via explicit git mv commands (no copy-and-delete, no compatibility shim).
No stale flat baseline files remain under .cloverleaf/baselines/ after migration (only subdirectory-organized files exist).
All compareVisual call-sites in reference-impl (prompt and lib) that construct a baselinePath are updated to include the {browser} path segment: .cloverleaf/baselines/{browser}/{slug}-{viewport}.png.
Attachment label paths in any UI Reviewer output schema or prompt that reference baseline filenames are updated to the new subdir form.
A migration guide or comment documents that the flat layout is deprecated and that new baselines must be placed under baselines/{browser}/.
acceptance_criteria: After migration, 'git status' shows only R (rename) entries for the moved baseline files, no untracked flat PNG files remain in .cloverleaf/baselines/ root.
Running the UI Reviewer against an existing task that previously produced .cloverleaf/baselines/faq-desktop.png now produces .cloverleaf/baselines/chromium/faq-desktop.png.
compareVisual called with browser="chromium", slug="faq", viewport="desktop" constructs path .cloverleaf/baselines/chromium/faq-desktop.png.
compareVisual called with browser="webkit", slug="faq", viewport="mobile" constructs path .cloverleaf/baselines/webkit/faq-mobile.png.
No references to the old flat path pattern ({slug}-{viewport}.png directly under .cloverleaf/baselines/) remain in reference-impl source files.

tasks.title: UI Reviewer prompt: 3-browser outer loop, per-engine escalation, axe chromium-only, maxCombinations cap
definition_of_done: reference-impl/prompts/ui-reviewer.md iterates 'browsers' as the outermost loop, wrapping the existing viewport × route loops, using the engines listed in config.browsers.

When a required browser binary is absent, the reviewer returns verdict 'escalate' with a finding that names the missing engine and includes the install command ('npx playwright install webkit firefox'; on Linux additionally 'npx playwright install-deps webkit').
axe-core scanning is executed on exactly the engine specified by config.axe.browser (defaulting to 'chromium'); webkit and firefox runs produce no axe output and no axe findings.

maxCombinations enforcement: when routes × viewports × browsers exceeds config.maxCombinations, the reviewer sorts affected routes by diff size (most-changed first), processes the first floor(maxCombinations / (viewports × browsers)) routes, and emits one 'warning'-severity finding with rule 'ui-review-cap' per skipped route, including the route name and the current vs cap count.
Per-browser baseline paths use the new .cloverleaf/baselines/{browser}/{slug}-{viewport}.png convention (consistent with CLV-17).
The prompt's step structure and output schema remain compatible with the existing feedback envelope (no Standard schema changes).
acceptance_criteria: A task with config.browsers=["chromium","webkit","firefox"] causes the reviewer to launch three separate browser sessions, one per engine, producing baselines under baselines/chromium/, baselines/webkit/, and baselines/firefox/.
A task run with webkit binary absent returns verdict 'escalate' with a finding message containing 'webkit' and 'npx playwright install webkit firefox'.
A task run with config.axe.browser="chromium" produces axe findings only from the chromium run; the webkit and firefox sections of the output contain no axe findings.

A task with 4 affected routes, 3 viewports, and 3 browsers (36 combinations) under a maxCombinations of 27 (floor(27/(3×3))=3 routes) skips 1 route and emits exactly 1 'warning' finding with rule 'ui-review-cap' for the skipped route.
Removing 'webkit' from config.browsers causes zero webkit baseline files to be written and zero webkit findings to be emitted.
The reviewer output passes feedback envelope schema validation after the changes.

tasks.title: Baseline-approval sidecar: state.json gate, approve-baselines skill, ui-review→qa block
definition_of_done: The UI Reviewer prompt writes .cloverleaf/runs/{taskId}/ui-review/state.json containing {"baselines_pending": true} whenever any compareVisual result is 'new-baseline' or 'dimension-mismatch'.

The cloverleaf-ui-review skill reads state.json after the agent completes; if baselines_pending is true it leaves the task in ui-review status and surfaces a human-readable message explaining that baseline approval is required before advancing to qa.
A new 'approve-baselines' reference-impl skill is implemented that writes {"baselines_pending": false} to state.json and then allows the ui-review → qa transition to proceed.
When no new-baseline or dimension-mismatch results are produced, state.json either contains {"baselines_pending": false} or is absent, and the ui-review → qa transition proceeds normally.
No Standard schemas (task.schema.json, state-machine JSON) are modified.
The approve-baselines skill is documented with its trigger condition and effect.
acceptance_criteria: Running the UI Reviewer on a task that produces at least one new-baseline result leaves the task in ui-review status and the skill output message contains the phrase 'baselines_pending' or an equivalent human-readable baseline approval notice.
Running approve-baselines on the same task writes baselines_pending: false to state.json and the subsequent ui-review → qa transition succeeds.

Running the UI Reviewer on a task that produces only 'match' or 'diff' results (no new-baseline or dimension-mismatch) does not set baselines_pending: true; the task advances to qa without requiring approve-baselines.
The Standard task state-machine JSON and task.schema.json are byte-for-byte identical before and after this task's implementation (no Standard changes).
The state.json sidecar file is co-located at .cloverleaf/runs/{taskId}/ui-review/state.json for a task with id 'CLV-42' results in path .cloverleaf/runs/CLV-42/ui-review/state.json.

tasks.title: End-to-end integration test: 3-browser matrix, per-browser baselines, engine diffs, baseline-approval gate
definition_of_done: A reference-impl integration test (e.g. in reference-impl/tests/) exercises the full cross-browser UI review flow against a minimal toy site change, using config.browsers=["chromium","webkit","firefox"] and config.maxCombinations=90.
The test asserts that per-browser baseline PNG files are written under .cloverleaf/baselines/chromium/, .cloverleaf/baselines/webkit/, and .cloverleaf/baselines/firefox/.
The test asserts that axe findings appear only in the chromium section of the output.
The test asserts that when baselines_pending is true, the task remains in ui-review status; after approve-baselines, the task advances to qa.
The test asserts that a browser-specific visual diff (e.g. a deliberate webkit-only rendering difference) surfaces as a finding attributed to the webkit engine.
The test is included in the reference-impl test suite (e.g. referenced in package.json test script or CI workflow) and passes in CI with all three browsers installed.
acceptance_criteria: 'npm test' (or equivalent) in reference-impl/ runs the integration test and it passes when chromium, webkit, and firefox are installed.
The integration test output confirms baseline PNGs exist at .cloverleaf/baselines/chromium/*.png, .cloverleaf/baselines/webkit/*.png, and .cloverleaf/baselines/firefox/*.png after the run.
The integration test confirms zero axe findings are emitted for webkit and firefox runs.
The integration test confirms the task status is 'ui-review' (not 'qa') after a run that produces new-baseline results, and 'qa' after approve-baselines is called.
The integration test confirms that a maxCombinations cap of 3 (below the toy route×viewport×browser product) causes at least one 'ui-review-cap' warning finding to appear in the output.
The integration test can be run in isolation without affecting other test suites.
